examples.text: وَأَمَّا قَوْلُ ٱلْعَرَبِ: «اِتَّقَى ٱللَّهَ ٱمْرُؤٌ فَعَلَ خَيْرًا يُثَبْ عَلَيْهِ» بِٱلْجَزْمِ، فَوَجْهُهُ أَنَّ «ٱتَّقَى ٱللَّهَ» وَ«فَعَلَ خَيْرًا» وَإِنْ كَانَا فِعْلَيْنِ مَاضِيَيْنِ ظَاهِرُهُمَا ٱلْخَبَرُ، إِلَّا أَنَّ ٱلْمُرَادَ بِهِمَا ٱلطَّلَبُ، وَٱلْمَعْنَى: لِيَتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ ٱمْرُؤٌ وَلْيَفْعَلْ خَيْرًا، وَكَذَٰلِكَ قَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿یَـٰۤأَیُّهَا ٱلَّذِینَ ءَامَنُوا۟ هَلۡ أَدُلُّكُمۡ عَلَىٰ تِجَـٰرَةࣲ تُنجِیكُم مِّنۡ عَذَابٍ أَلِیمࣲ ۝١٠ تُؤۡمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ وَتُجَـٰهِدُونَ فِی سَبِیلِ ٱللَّهِ بِأَمۡوَ ٰ⁠لِكُمۡ وَأَنفُسِكُمۡۚ ذَ ٰ⁠لِكُمۡ خَیۡرࣱ لَّكُمۡ إِن كُنتُمۡ تَعۡلَمُونَ ۝١١ یَغۡفِرۡ لَكُمۡ﴾ فَجَزْمُ يَغْفِرْ لِأَنَّهُ جَوَابٌ لِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿تُؤۡمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ وَتُجَـٰهِدُونَ﴾ لِكَوْنِهِ فِي مَعْنَى «آمِنُوا وَجَاهِدُوا»، وَلَيْسَ جَوَابًا لِلِٱسْتِفْهَامِ، لِأَنَّ غُفْرَانَ ٱلذُّنُوبِ لَا يَتَسَبَّبُ عَنْ نَفْسِ ٱلدَّلَالَةِ، بَلْ عَنِ ٱلْإِيمَانِ وَٱلْجِهَادِ.

body.text: It is in the mind of such a one to do so and so
]. (Aboo-Is-hák, M.) Some of the lexicologists assert the
to be one and the same, except that the former is fem., and the latter [generally or often] masc.: others say, that the latter is that whereby is life; and the former,
so that when one sleeps, God takes away his
, which is not taken save at death: and the
is thus called because of its connexion with the
], which quits him when he sleeps, so that he does not understand thereby, God taking it away: (Zj:) and
], and when this quits him, the breath quits with it; whereas the sleeper breathes: and this is the difference between the taking away of the
of the sleeper in sleep and the taking away of the
of the living [at death.] (Zj.) Much has been said respecting the
; whether they be one, or different: but the truth is, that there is a difference between them, since they are not always interchangeable: for it is said in the Kur, [xv. 29 and xxxviii. 72,]
[And I have blown into him of my spirit.]; not
, nor would this expression be well except from Jesus: and [lviii. 9,]
And they say in their souls,
]: for which it would not be well to say
]; for which no Arab would say
: hence, the difference between them depends upon the considerations of relation: and this is indicated by a trad., in which it is said that God created Adam, and put into him a

; and that from the latter was his quality of abstaining from unlawful and indecorous things, and his understanding, and his clemency, or forbearance, and his liberality, and his fidelity; and from the former, [which is also called
,] his appetence, and his unsteadiness, and his hastiness of disposition, and his anger: therefore one should not say that
. (R.) The Arabs also make the discriminative

to be two; because it sometimes commands the man to do a thing or forbids him to do it; and this is on the occasion of setting about an affair that is disliked: therefore they make that which commands him to be a
, and that which forbids him to be as though it were another
: and hence the saying, mentioned by Z,
Such a one consults his two souls,
]; said of a man when two opinions occur to him. (TA.) [

body.text_bw: taEolamu maA fiY nafosiY walaA A^aEolamu maA fiY nafosika
body.text_ar: تَعْلَمُ مَا فِى نَفْسِى وَلَا اَعْلَمُ مَا فِى نَفْسِكَ

body.text: Thou knowest what is in myself,
in my essence, and I know not what is in thyself,
and I know not what is in thyself,
in thine essence, nor that whereof Thou hast the knowledge,
what Thou concealest of the things which Thou knowest;
(Bd, Jel;) so that the interpretation is,
Thou knowest what I know, and I know not what Thou knowest:

body.text: Put thou thy mouth into the vessel and drink
I drank a gulp, and gulps:

body.text_ar: اَجِدُ نَفَسَ رَبَِّكُمْ مِنْ قِبَلِ اليَمَنِ

body.text: I perceive your Lord's removal of grief,

meaning, through the aid and hospitality of the people of El-Medeeneh, who were of El-Yemen; (K, TA;) i. e., of the Ansár, who were of [the tribe of] El-Azd, from ElYemen. (TA.) It is [said by some to be] a metaphor, from
, which the act of breathing draws back into the inside, so that its heat becomes cooled and moderated: or from
, which one scents, so that thereby he refreshes himself: or from
